title: A street I miss
summary: Describe a street from your past and compare it to what it is like now.
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a street you miss. Describe what was on it in the past and compare it to what it is like now. Use past simple for what you remember and present simple for what it is like today. Say why you miss it.
hintStarters: There was a street near my old home that I still think about…
When I was young, that street had many things that are gone now…
Now the street looks completely different because…

hintVocab.meaningVi: cảm thấy quen thuộc, như ở nhà

sampleText: There was a narrow street called Rue des Épices near my grandmother's house in Marrakech. I loved that street when I was a child. On both sides, there were small stalls selling spices, dried fruit, and leather bags. The smell of cumin and cinnamon was always in the air. Old men sat on wooden chairs outside their shops and talked to everyone who walked past. Children played on the pavement in the evenings, and the sound of their voices filled the whole street. I used to go there every Saturday with my cousin to buy fresh bread from a baker at the end of the road. Now the street is very different. Many of the old stalls are gone and a new block of flats stands where the baker used to be. The pavement is cleaner and wider, but it no longer feels the same. When I visit Marrakech today, I walk down that street and I do not see any familiar faces. The people who made that street feel alive are no longer there. I miss the noise, the smell, and the feeling that everyone knew each other.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: con đường hẹp tên Rue des Épices

sampleAnnotations.phrase: The smell of cumin and cinnamon was always in the air
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Mùi thì là và quế lúc nào cũng lan trong không khí

sampleAnnotations.phrase: Old men sat on wooden chairs outside their shops
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Những người đàn ông lớn tuổi ngồi trên ghế gỗ trước cửa hàng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I used to go there every Saturday
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi hay đến đó mỗi thứ Bảy

sampleAnnotations.phrase: block of flats stands where the baker used to be
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một tòa chung cư mọc lên chỗ tiệm bánh mì ngày xưa

sampleAnnotations.phrase: it no longer feels the same
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: nó không còn cảm giác như trước nữa

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I do not see any familiar faces
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi không thấy khuôn mặt quen thuộc nào

sampleAnnotations.phrase: The people who made that street feel alive
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Những người khiến con đường đó sống động

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I miss the noise, the smell, and the feeling
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi nhớ tiếng ồn, mùi hương và cảm giác đó

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple vs present simple for comparison
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng past simple để mô tả những gì đã có ('there were', 'sat', 'played', 'used to go') và present simple để mô tả tình trạng hiện tại ('is', 'are gone', 'stands', 'do not see'). Sự đối lập giữa hai thì tạo ra hiệu ứng so sánh.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use past simple to describe what existed before ('there were', 'sat', 'played', 'used to go') and present simple for the current state ('is', 'are gone', 'stands', 'do not see'). Contrasting the two tenses creates the comparison effect.

sampleTranslationVi: Có một con đường hẹp tên Rue des Épices gần nhà bà tôi ở Marrakech. Tôi yêu con đường đó khi còn nhỏ. Ở cả hai phía, có những quầy hàng nhỏ bán gia vị, trái cây khô và túi da. Mùi thì là và quế lúc nào cũng lan trong không khí. Những người đàn ông lớn tuổi ngồi trên ghế gỗ trước cửa hàng và nói chuyện với tất cả mọi người đi ngang qua. Bọn trẻ chơi trên vỉa hè vào buổi tối, tiếng chúng vang khắp con đường. Tôi hay đến đó mỗi thứ Bảy cùng anh họ để mua bánh mì tươi từ một tiệm bánh cuối đường. Bây giờ con đường đã rất khác. Nhiều quầy hàng cũ đã biến mất và một tòa chung cư mới mọc lên chỗ tiệm bánh mì ngày xưa. Vỉa hè sạch hơn và rộng hơn, nhưng nó không còn cảm giác như trước nữa. Khi tôi ghé thăm Marrakech ngày nay, tôi đi trên con đường đó và không thấy khuôn mặt quen thuộc nào. Những người khiến con đường đó sống động không còn ở đó nữa. Tôi nhớ tiếng ồn, mùi hương và cảm giác rằng mọi người đều biết nhau.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'On both sides, ___ small stalls selling spices.'
options: there was
there were
there are
there had
mcQuestions.explanation: 'There were' is past simple plural, correct for describing what existed in the past.
mcQuestions.hint: The subject 'stalls' is plural, and the description is in the past.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'used to' for a past habit?
options: I used to went there every Saturday with my cousin.
I was used to go there every Saturday with my cousin.
I used to go there every Saturday with my cousin.
I use to go there every Saturday with my cousin.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Used to + base verb' expresses a repeated past action. Never 'used to went'.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Used to' is always followed by the base verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct present simple sentence to describe how a place is today.
options: Many of the old stalls were gone.
Many of the old stalls is gone.
Many of the old stalls has gone.
Many of the old stalls are gone.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple 'are gone' describes the current state of the street.
mcQuestions.hint: The subject 'stalls' is plural and the situation is current.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option correctly uses 'no longer' to show something has stopped?
options: it no longer feels the same
it not longer feels the same
it no more feels the same
it doesn't longer feel the same
mcQuestions.explanation: 'No longer' is the correct phrase meaning something has stopped being true.
mcQuestions.hint: The phrase is 'no longer', not 'not longer' or 'no more'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct comparative sentence at A2 level.
options: The pavement is more clean and more wide, but it no longer feels the same.
The pavement is cleaner and wider, but it no longer feels the same.
The pavement was cleaner and wider, but it no longer feels the same.
The pavement is cleaner and wider, although it feels the same.
mcQuestions.explanation: Short adjectives add '-er' for comparative: cleaner, wider. Present simple 'is' is correct here.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses past simple for completed actions?
options: Old men were sit on wooden chairs outside their shops.
Old men sitting on wooden chairs outside their shops.
Old men sat on wooden chairs outside their shops.
Old men has sat on wooden chairs outside their shops.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Sat' is the past simple of 'sit', correct for describing a recurring past scene.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the past simple of 'sit'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best contrasts past and present using 'but'?
options: It was noisy and full of life, because now it is quiet.
It was noisy and full of life, so now it is quiet.
It was noisy and full of life, although now it is quiet.
It was noisy and full of life, but now it is quiet.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'But' introduces a contrast between two opposite states.
mcQuestions.hint: Which linker shows a contrast between two opposite ideas?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase correctly describes where a new building stands today?
options: a block of flats stands where the baker used to be
a block of flats stands where the baker use to be
a block of flats stood where the baker used to be
a block of flats is stand where the baker used to be
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple 'stands' describes where the building is now. 'Used to be' is the correct past form.
mcQuestions.hint: The building is there now (present simple), and the baker was there before ('used to be').

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural way to describe a sensory memory of a place.
options: The smell of cumin and cinnamon were always in the air.
The smell of cumin and cinnamon was always in the air.
The smell of cumin and cinnamon is always in the air back then.
The smell of cumin and cinnamon has always been in the air.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'The smell' is singular, so 'was' is correct. Past simple for a past memory.
mcQuestions.hint: 'The smell' is singular — which past form of 'be' is correct?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase means a building was demolished?
options: torn up
torn off
torn down
torn apart
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Torn down' means a building was demolished or destroyed.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrasal verb means to demolish a building?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly expresses missing a place?
options: I miss the noise, the smell, and the feeling that everyone know each other.
I missed the noise, the smell, and the feeling that everyone knew each other.
I am missing the noise, the smell, and the feeling that everyone knew each other.
I miss the noise, the smell, and the feeling that everyone knew each other.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple 'miss' expresses a current feeling. The past clause uses 'knew'.
mcQuestions.hint: The feeling of missing exists now; what people did is in the past.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Do you think it is always bad when old streets and buildings are changed or replaced? What is lost, and what might be gained?
